\begin{align}
 & \begin{cases}
I_{1}=G_{11}U_{1}+G_{12}U_{2}+I_{S_{1}} \\
I_{2}=G_{21}U_{1}+G_{22}U_{2}+I_{S_{2}} \\

  \\ 
 & \begin{cases}
U_{1}=R_{11}I_{1}+R_{12}J_{2}+E_{O_{1}} \\
U_{2}=R_{21}I_{1}+R_{12}I_{2}+E_{O_{2}}
\end{cases} \\
 & I_{1}=I_{S_{1}} |\begin{matrix}
  \\
U_{1}=0 \\
U_{2}=0
\end{matrix}
\end{align}

$$
\begin{cases}
U_{1}=8J_{1}+\cancel{ 1J_{2} }+6 \implies I_{1}=-0.75A \\
U_{2}=1J_{1}+\cancel{ 1J_{2} }-4 = -4.75V
\end{cases}
$$
amp meter is short circuit
volt meter is open circuit
$U_{1}=0$
$I_{2}=0$

# Lecture 6 Analysis of complex electric circuit (Nodal analysis method)
![[Pasted image 20251125131839.png]]
we formulate (node count -1 ) equasions
Ⓐ $I_{5}-I_{1}-I_{6}=0$
Ⓑ $I_{1}+I_{4}-I_{2}-0$
Ⓒ $I_{2}+I_{6}-I_{3}=0$
meshes = number of branches+number of nodes-1
(Ⅰ)  $E_{6}+U_{2}-E_{1}-U_{6}=0$
(Ⅱ) $E_{1}+U_{4}-E_{3}-U_{5}=0$
(Ⅲ) $V_{3}+E_{4}-V_{4}-U_{2}=0$ 
there's no link betwenn voltages and currents so we can't find solution (udr ohms law)

$\overbrace{ I_{6}=\frac{U_{6}}{R_{6}} }^{ ABC } \ \overbrace{ U_{6}=I_{6}R_{6} }^{\text{I II III}}$
$I_{4}=\frac{U_{4}}{R_{4}} \ U_{4}=I_{4}R_{4}$
$I_{5}=\frac{U_{5}}{R_{5}} \ U_{5}=I_{5}R_{5}$
$I_{2}=\frac{U_{2}}{R_{2}} \ U_{2}=I_{2}R_{2}$

standard method doesn't work well with this problem
Coutri method

$V_{A}=0$
$V_{B}=E_{1}$ (this is the assumption)
$V_{C}\left( \frac{1}{R_{2}}+\frac{1}{R_{6}} \right)-V_{B}\left( \frac{1}{R_{2}} \right)-V_{D}(0)=\frac{E_{6}}{R_{6}}-I_{3}$
$V_{D}\left( \frac{1}{R_{4}}+\frac{1}{R_{5}} \right)-V_{B}\left( \frac{1}{R_{4}} \right)-V_{C}(0)=-\frac{E_{4}}{R_{4}}+I_{3}$

$U_{3}=V_{D}-V_{C}$
$U_{2}=V_{B}-V_{C}$

$V_{D}+E_{4}-U_{4}=V_{B}$
$U_{4}=V_{D}-V_{B}+E_{4}$

---

Example (forgor the drawing lol)
Calculate the ideal voltmeter reading and the power delivered by the current source. Data:
$G_{1}=1mS$
$G_{2}=1mS$
$G_{3}=4mS$
$G_{4}=2mS$
$G_{5}=8mS$
$G_{6}=3mS$
$I_{1}=48mA$
$E_{1}=4V$

D to C is resitance branch so we should ground one of them (we prefer positive numbers so we ground )
$V_{D}=0$
{
$V_{C}=E_{1}=4V$
$V_{A}(G_{1}+G_{4}+G_{6})-V_{B}(G_{4})-V_{C}(G_{6})=0$
$V_{B}(G_{4}+G_{5})-V_{A}(G_{4})-V_{C}(G_{5})=J_{1}$
}

$V_{A}·6·10^{-3}-V_{B}·2·10^{3}-4·3·10^{-3}=0$
$V_{B}·10·10^{-3}-V_{A}·2·10^{-3}-4·8·10^{-3}=48·10^{-3}$
cancel $10^{-3}$
{
$6V_{A}-2V_{B}=12 \ /·5$
$-2V_{A}+10V_{B}=80$
{
$30V_{A}-10V_{B}=60$
$-2V_{A}+10V_{B}=80$
$28V_{A}=140$
$V_{A}=5V$
$V_{B}=9V$

Voltage reading is 5V

$-U_{3}+U_{1}=V_{B}$
$U_{3}=\frac{I_{1}}{G_{3}}=\frac{48mA}{4mS}=12V$

---

$V_{D}=0$
$V_{C}=5$
$V_{B}=5$
$V_{A}\left( \frac{1}{R_{1}}+\frac{1}{400}+\frac{1}{R_{2}+R_{3}} \right)-V_{B}\left( \frac{1}{R_{1}} \right)-V_{C}\left( \frac{1}{400}+\frac{1}{R_{2}+R_{3}} \right)=\frac{8}{R_{2}+R_{3}} \ /·400$
$4V_{A}-10-10=-8$
$4V_{A}=12$

$U_{R_{1}}=V_{A}-V_{B}=-2V$
$P_{R_{1}}=\frac{4}{200}=\underline{20mW}$

# Part 2

$$
\begin{align}
 & V_{D}=0 \\
  & \begin{cases}
 V_{C}=10V \\
  V_{A}\left( \frac{1}{100}+\frac{1}{100} \right)-V_{B}·0= \frac{20}{100}+I \\
  V_{B}\left( \frac{1}{100} \right)-V_{A}(0)-V_{C}\left( \frac{1}{100} \right)=0.1-I  \\
 V_{A}=V_{B}+10
\end{cases}
\end{align}
$$

## 2.Piece-Wise-Linear (PWL) based method (approximation)

$I_{N}=\frac{E_{0}-U_{N}}{R_{t}}=-\frac{1}{R_{t}}U_{N}+\frac{E_{0}}{R_{t}}$
